skill.name: webapp-testing
skill.description: Build and maintain Playwright tests for web applications, including browser automation, form validation, API mocking, mobile emulation, screenshots, and page objects.
skill.summary: Create practical Playwright test suites for web application workflows.
skill.author: AutumnsGrove

security_audit.summary: The static scanner found many command, network, storage, filesystem, and heuristic combination patterns, but most are normal Playwright examples or testing utilities rather than malicious behavior. No prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, or hostile endpoint was found. The skill still has medium risk because browser automation can capture screenshots, inspect storage, intercept network requests, and run developer test commands against real web sessions.

medium_findings.title: Browser Storage and Cookie Access
medium_findings.description: The helper includes methods that read and modify cookies and localStorage. This is expected for Playwright testing, but it can expose session tokens if users run tests against authenticated production sites.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly interacts with browser cookies and localStorage. The intent is legitimate test isolation, but the capability is sensitive in authenticated sessions.

medium_findings.title: Screenshot and Video Capture Can Record Sensitive UI
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally supports screenshots, videos, and trace files for debugging. This is legitimate test functionality, but captured artifacts can contain private user data, tokens, or customer information.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly document and implement screenshot capture. The risk depends on target data, so this is a controlled but real exposure concern.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic JavaScript Construction in Helper Methods
medium_findings.description: Several helper methods interpolate selector, text, or storage-key values into JavaScript strings passed to browser evaluation. This is common in test helpers, but unsafe if those values come from untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation pattern is visible and can execute malformed JavaScript in the browser context. The likely inputs are developer-controlled selectors, which lowers severity.

low_findings.title: Documented Test Commands Trigger Static Command Alerts
low_findings.description: Many external command detections occur inside Markdown examples for installing Playwright, running pytest, or starting a local web server. These are normal developer instructions, not hidden command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are documentation or configuration snippets for standard Playwright workflows. No runtime wrapper secretly invokes shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Example Targets
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings use example.com, localhost, Playwright documentation, or reserved documentation domains. I did not find evidence that the skill exfiltrates data to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are conventional sample destinations used in test examples. Network interception is a core Playwright capability and not evidence of malicious networking by itself.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is CI Configuration
low_findings.description: The environment variable findings appear in Playwright CI configuration examples such as process.env.CI. No evidence found of reading secrets or transmitting environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected environment variable is used for normal CI behavior selection. There is no paired secret access or network exfiltration in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Mobile Device Names
low_findings.description: Several weak cryptography alerts are caused by strings such as iPhone 12 or Pixel 5 in mobile emulation examples. These are device presets, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines reference Playwright mobile device presets and viewport setup. They do not implement hashing, encryption, or certificate validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Artifact Capture
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can create screenshots and related test artifacts. Users should avoid capturing real secrets or production customer data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Screenshot creation is directly implemented. The security impact depends on the pages under test.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Interception
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper can intercept, mock, abort, and monitor browser requests. This is expected for API testing, but it should be limited to authorized test environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Network interception is explicitly implemented and documented. No malicious destination was found, but the capability changes browser traffic.

dangerous_patterns.title: JavaScript String Interpolation Before Browser Evaluation
dangerous_patterns.description: Helper methods build JavaScript strings with interpolated selector or key values. Prefer Playwright APIs or argument passing when values are not hardcoded.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is direct and repeatable in helper methods. It is medium risk because typical selectors are developer-authored.

content.value_statement: Web teams need reliable browser tests that cover real user flows without excessive setup time. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate Playwright tests, helper utilities, and testing guidance.
actual_capabilities: Generate Python Playwright tests for navigation, login, forms, and checkout workflows.
Show patterns for API mocking, request interception, and network request monitoring.
Provide mobile emulation examples for iPhone, Pixel, tablet, and custom viewport testing.
Create page object model structures for maintainable test suites.
Provide helper utilities for screenshots, assertions, form filling, cookies, and storage cleanup.
Guide CI configuration for pytest, browser selection, screenshots, videos, and retries.
limitations: It does not verify that generated selectors match a live application without running tests.
It does not replace manual security review for authenticated or production test sessions.
Visual comparison support is a placeholder unless users add an image comparison library.
Examples use sample domains and must be adapted to each project.

use_cases.title: Build Regression Tests
use_cases.description: Create stable Playwright tests for login, navigation, forms, and checkout paths before each release.

use_cases.title: Add Browser Automation
use_cases.description: Convert manual verification steps into repeatable browser automation with clear assertions and setup.

use_cases.title: Prepare CI Test Runs
use_cases.description: Configure cross-browser test execution, retries, screenshots, videos, and reports for pipeline runs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Python Playwright test for this page: [URL]. Verify the title, main heading, and one primary button.

prompt_templates.title: Test a Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Playwright tests for this form flow. Include valid submission, required-field errors, invalid input, and success confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Mock an API
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Playwright tests that mock these API responses: [endpoints]. Verify loading, success, empty, and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a maintainable Playwright test suite for this web app. Include page objects, fixtures, CI settings, and data cleanup.

output_examples.input: Test the login flow for a web application.
output_examples.output: A Playwright test plan with setup, form filling steps, assertions for successful login, error-state checks, and session cleanup guidance.

output_examples.input: Add mobile responsive tests for a product page.
output_examples.output: A set of mobile viewport scenarios that check navigation, layout changes, touch interactions, and screenshots for review.

output_examples.input: Mock product API failures during checkout.
output_examples.output: A network interception approach that simulates success, delay, and failure responses, with assertions for loading and error messages.

best_practices: Use stable selectors such as roles, labels, and test IDs instead of brittle DOM paths.
Run tests against authorized staging or local environments when screenshots, cookies, or storage may contain sensitive data.
Keep tests isolated by clearing cookies, localStorage, and test data between scenarios.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated tests against production accounts that contain real customer or secret data.
Do not rely on fixed sleeps when Playwright auto-waiting or explicit assertions can wait for the state.
Do not pass untrusted strings into helper methods that build JavaScript for page.evaluate.

faq.question: What testing framework does this skill use?
faq.answer: It focuses on Playwright, with examples mainly written for Python and pytest.

faq.question: Can it test mobile layouts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes mobile emulation patterns for Playwright device presets and custom viewports.

faq.question: Can it mock backend APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides examples for intercepting, fulfilling, aborting, and monitoring browser network requests.

faq.question: Does it create visual regression tests?
faq.answer: It can create screenshot workflows. Full image comparison requires adding a comparison library.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with authenticated sessions?
faq.answer: Use caution. Screenshots, cookies, localStorage, videos, and traces can contain sensitive session data.

faq.question: Does it support CI pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for pytest settings, browser selection, retries, videos, screenshots, and local server startup.
